headline: Global Bonds Jump as Ceasefire Sows Doubts Over Steep Rate Hikes
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Global bonds surged Wednesday on news that the US and Iran had agreed to a ceasefire, pausing a war that roiled markets for weeks by delivering the worst oil shock in years. European debt, at the heart of the selloff due to the region’s exposure to soaring energy prices, led the gains with some yields falling more than 25 basis points as traders slashed wagers on interest-rate hikes. As part of the two-week truce, Tehran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial transit rou

European debt, at the heart of the selloff due to the region’s exposure to soaring energy prices, led the gains with some yields falling more than 25 basis points as traders slashed wagers on interest-rate hikes. headline: Delta Air Lines Flags $2 Billion Iran Fuel Hit. Why the Stock Is Flying After Earnings.
summary: Delta Air Lines stock was surging ahead of the open Wednesday as the carrier’s earnings and the two-week U.S., Iran cease-fire deal proved to be tailwinds for the shares.  Demand is so strong that Delta expects to post a $1 billion pretax profit in the current quarter despite a more than $2 billion increase in fuel costs.  The carrier is also cutting capacity to mitigate the impact of higher fuel prices.

content.title: Delta Air Lines Flags $2 Billion Iran Fuel Hit. Why the Stock Is Flying After Earnings.
content.summary: Delta Air Lines stock was surging ahead of the open Wednesday as the carrier’s earnings and the two-week U.S., Iran cease-fire deal proved to be tailwinds for the shares.  Demand is so strong that Delta expects to post a $1 billion pretax profit in the current quarter despite a more than $2 billion increase in fuel costs.  The carrier is also cutting capacity to mitigate the impact of higher fuel prices.

headline: Exxon Sees 6% of Its Worldwide Output Shut on Mideast Conflict
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Exxon Mobil Corp. said 6% of its global first-quarter production was knocked out as the Iran war paralyzed much of the Persian Gulf energy industry.Half of those outages were concentrated at a liquefied natural gas complex in Qatar in which Exxon is a partner, the company said on Wednesday. Two LNG production lines, or trains, were damaged.“Public reports indicate the damage will take a prolonged period to repair,” Exxon said in a statement. “Pending an on-site evaluation, we are

headline: Jamie Dimon Basically Says: Some People In Private Credit Have No Business Being In Private Credit
summary: As risks quietly build across the $3 trillion private credit market, Jamie Dimon is warning that the next downturn could expose weaker lenders—though regulators say the fallout is unlikely to threaten the broader financial system. The JPMorgan CEO, in a letter to shareholders, wrote that "it's always been true that not everyone providing credit is necessarily good at it," when speaking about the current private credit environment. "There are many players who are late to this game, and it should

headline: Bond Traders Boost Bets on a Fed Cut This Year After Ceasefire
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Bond traders boosted wagers that the Federal Reserve will lower interest rates this year as a US-Iran ceasefire sent oil tumbling.Treasuries rallied along with other government debt markets, paring losses since the war began Feb. 28. The energy price surge it caused threatened to stoke inflation and cause central banks either to delay interest-rate cuts or to raise rates.In the case of the Fed, the oil shock evaporated expectations for two-quarter point cuts this year, and briefly

headline: Why The Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM) Story Is Shifting With New Policy And Valuation Views
summary: Archer-Daniels-Midland’s fair value estimate has been updated to about US$63.82 from roughly US$62.09, a modest shift that puts fresh attention on how analysts are recalibrating expectations. Several firms are pairing this with higher price targets, such as moves to US$68 from US$61 and JPMorgan’s US$4 increase, as they weigh policy support, commodity trends, and a mixed sector backdrop. Read on to see how to track these changing views and what to watch as the story continues to evolve. Stay...

headline: Exxon Sees First-Quarter Production Loss of 6% on Iran War
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Exxon Mobil Corp. lost 6% of its global production in the first quarter as the Iran war paralyzed oil and natural gas operations in the Persian Gulf.The Strait of Hormuz’s closure over the past five weeks have severely affected Exxon’s operations in the United Arab Emirates and Qatar, where it owns stakes in the giant Las Raffan liquefied natural gas complex, the company said in a statement Wednesday. Those two nations account for about 20% of Exxon’s global annual production. Exx

headline: Evolving Narrative For Tesco (LSE:TSCO) As Analysts Weigh Upside Against Execution Risks
